author: Dr. Rachel Kim
content:
URGENT CARE VISIT — 10/22/2021, 6:45 PM
Patient: Sandoval, Maria Isabel (Age 18)
Pay status: Self-pay; sliding-scale arrangement.
Interpreter: Spanish phone interpreter

CHIEF COMPLAINT:
2 days of burning with urination.

HPI:
18-year-old female with 2 days of dysuria and increased urinary frequency. Some suprapubic discomfort. Denies fever, chills, flank pain, hematuria, vaginal discharge. Last menstrual period 1 week ago, normal. Sexually active with one partner over the past 6 months; condom use inconsistent. No prior UTIs. No diabetes.

ROS: GU — as above. Constitutional — denies fever. Otherwise negative.

VITALS: T 98.6 F, HR 84, BP 112/70, RR 14, SpO2 99% RA. Weight 53.4 kg.

EXAM:
General: Comfortable.
Abdomen: Soft. Mild suprapubic tenderness. No CVA tenderness bilaterally.
No other relevant findings.

TESTING:
UA dipstick: positive leukocyte esterase, positive nitrites, no blood, no protein.
Urine pregnancy test: NEGATIVE.

IMPRESSION / PLAN:
Uncomplicated lower UTI.
1. Nitrofurantoin 100 mg PO BID x 5 days. Cash-pay $12 at discount pharmacy.
2. Push fluids. APAP PRN.
3. Return for fever, flank pain, vomiting, or worsening symptoms despite abx.
4. Counseled re: post-coital voiding, hydration. Offered STI testing — patient declined today, will consider for annual visit.

Note: patient lost Medicaid in 2020. Reviewed Esperanza Health (FQHC) intake options for ongoing PCP. Provided written referral.

Visit time: 25 min.
attestation:
Electronically signed by: Dr. Rachel Kim, MD
Date: 10/22/2021
